key_insight: Precision-aware PTME profiling shows that lightweight LLMs cannot be selected reliably by size, FLOPs, latency, or accuracy alone because different models trade off precision, time, memory, and energy in non-dominated ways.
engineering_relevance: For edge and local deployments, PTME measurements provide a practical decision framework for choosing the model that best fits a given resource envelope without assuming proxy metrics predict real inference quality.
method: Measure Precision, execution Time, peak Memory, and Energy directly at hardware level during local inference.
Evaluate a representative set of lightweight LLMs under edge-class resource envelopes on a controlled desktop platform.
Compare behavior across code generation, mathematical reasoning, and multi-task understanding benchmarks, then use Pareto analysis to identify non-dominated models.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that tighter resource envelopes increased cost without changing precision, with execution time affected more strongly than energy and larger models penalized the most.
watch_item: Because the study is performed on a controlled desktop setup, the PTME tradeoffs may shift on different hardware, workloads, or real mobile/edge devices.
